primary_accounting_issue: Éligibilité, en comptes consolidés, d’un dividende intragroupe ou de la créance de dividende correspondante comme élément couvert au titre du risque de change

authority_resolution.selection_reason: I applied the stated precedence rule by selecting the single document that directly governs hedge-accounting eligibility and hedged items for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements; IAS 39 addresses that issue explicitly, whereas IAS 21 only provides supporting foreign-exchange translation mechanics.
authority_resolution.residual_uncertainty: low — IAS 39 is the only document in the provided context that directly addresses whether intragroup items can qualify as hedged items in consolidated financial statements.

primary_authority.reason: These paragraphs directly govern hedge-accounting designation of hedging instruments and hedged items, including intragroup foreign currency exposures in consolidated financial statements.

supporting_authority.reason: These paragraphs clarify when intragroup monetary items create exchange differences that remain in consolidated financial statements and therefore can create genuine FX exposure.
This interpretation supports the distinct hedge-accounting model for net investments in foreign operations and clarifies the nature of the hedged FX risk.
This paragraph supports the consolidation backdrop by requiring elimination of intragroup balances and transactions, subject to the separate FX consequences addressed by IAS 21 and IAS 39.

peripheral_authority.reason: These paragraphs concern deferred tax consequences of investments and FX differences, not hedge-accounting eligibility for intragroup dividends.
These paragraphs concern related-party disclosures and elimination of intragroup balances, not hedge-accounting models.
These paragraphs address cash-flow classification and financing-liability disclosures, not designation of hedged items.

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for a recognised foreign-currency asset or liability
Hedge accounting for foreign-currency variability in future cash flows from a forecast transaction
Hedge accounting for foreign-currency exposure arising from a net investment in a foreign operation

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 identifies a recognised asset or liability as a hedged item, which makes this a peer hedge-accounting model once a dividend receivable exists.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 identifies a highly probable forecast transaction as a hedged item, including certain intragroup foreign-currency transactions in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRIC 16 identify net investment hedging as a distinct hedge-accounting model for foreign currency exposure in consolidated financial statements.
